Code fragment: dslContext.select(BUILDING.fields())
Explanation: Using `BUILDING.fields()` selects all columns from the BUILDING table, which can fetch unnecessary data and break if the schema changes. Replace with explicit column selection: `dslContext.select(BUILDING.ID, BUILDING.NAME, BUILDING.COUNTRY, BUILDING.CITY, BUILDING.STREET, BUILDING.NUMBER, BUILDING.AREA_ID)`.

Code fragment: return dslContext.select(BUILDING.fields())
Explanation: Using `BUILDING.fields()` selects all columns from the BUILDING table, which retrieves more data than necessary and couples the code to the table schema. Instead, explicitly select only the columns needed: `select(BUILDING.ID, BUILDING.NAME, BUILDING.COUNTRY, BUILDING.CITY, BUILDING.STREET, BUILDING.NUMBER, BUILDING.AREA_ID)`.

Code fragment: return dslContext.selectFrom(FACILITY_RESERVATION)
Explanation: Using selectFrom() fetches all columns from the FACILITY_RESERVATION table, which may retrieve unnecessary data and impact performance. Consider using an explicit select() with only the columns needed for checking overlapping reservations.

Code fragment: return dslContext.selectFrom(POLL_OPTION)
Explanation: Using selectFrom(POLL_OPTION) fetches all columns from the POLL_OPTION table, which may retrieve unnecessary data and reduce query performance. Consider using select() with only the specific columns needed for this operation.

Code fragment: return dslContext.selectFrom(table)
Explanation: Using selectFrom(table) fetches all columns from the APP_USER table, which may retrieve unnecessary data. Consider using an explicit select() with only the required columns, such as select(table.ID, table.USERNAME, ...).

Code fragment: return dslContext.selectFrom(table)
